lines.en_lit: Rabbi Ḥiyya, came in, returned to the beginning bar Kappara came in, returned to the beginning Rabbi Shimon, son of Rabbi came in, he returned to the beginning Rabbi Ḥanina bar Ḥama came in, said Shall I go back so many times? He did not return Rabbi Ḥanina was offended Rav went before on Yom Kippur eve thirteen but he would not be appeased.

How could act this way? Didn’t Rabbi Yosei bar Ḥanina say: Anyone who requests forgiveness from another should not ask more than three times? Rav is different, And how could Rabbi Ḥanina act this way Didn’t Rava say: anyone who suppresses his honor pardons all his sins?

Rather, Rabbi Ḥanina saw in a dream Rav was being hung on a palm tree, and learned anyone which he was being hung on a palm tree will become the head He said: Learn from this that he must eventually become the head I will not be appeased, so that he will have to go and study Torah in Babylonia.

The Sages taught: mitzva of confession Yom Kippur eve when darkness But the Sages said: confess before he eats and drinks lest he become confused at the meal, And although one confessed before he ate and drank, he confesses after he eats and drinks, perhaps he committed some sin during the meal And although one confessed the evening prayer he confess the morning prayer. morning prayer, he confess during additional prayer. during additional prayer, he confess during afternoon prayer; during afternoon prayer, he confess during closing prayer [ne’ila].

And where does one say An individual after his prayer, and the prayer leader says it in the middle What does one say; Rav said: You know the mysteries of the universe, And Shmuel said From the depths of the heart. And Levi said And in your Torah it is written, saying, Rabbi Yoḥanan said Master of the Universe.

Rabbi Yehuda said For our iniquities are too many to count and our sins are too great to number. Rav Hamnuna said: My God, before I was formed I was unworthy. Now that I have been formed, it is as if I had not been formed. I am dust while alive, how much more so when I am dead. See, I am before You like a vessel filled with shame and disgrace. May it be Your will that I may sin no more, and as for I have committed before You, erase in Your compassion, but not by suffering. This is the confession that Rava all year and that Rav Hamnuna Zuta on Yom Kippur.

Mar Zutra said: We said only when he did not say But we have sinned. However, he said But we have sinned, he need not anything further As bar Hamdudei said: I was standing before Shmuel and he was sitting; and when the prayer leader reached But we have sinned, stood. said: Learn from here that this is the main the confession,

We learned there, At three times in the year, priests raise their hands four times in day: In morning prayer, in additional prayer, in afternoon prayer, and at closing [ne’ila] of the gates. And these are three times During fasts and during watches [ma’amadot], and on Yom Kippur.

What is the closing of the gates, Rav said: an added prayer And Shmuel said: What are we, what are our lives? raises an objection the night of Yom Kippur, one prays seven and confesses; during morning prayer, one prays seven and confesses; during additional prayer, one prays seven and confesses; during afternoon prayer, one prays seven and confesses; during ne’ila prayer, one prays seven and confesses.

This is tanna’im as it was taught Yom Kippur, as darkness one prays seven and confesses and ends with the confession; the statement of Rabbi Meir. And the Rabbis say: He prays seven and if he wishes to end with a confession, he ends this is a refutation of Shmuel, a refutation.

Ulla bar Rav went down before Rava, He opened with: You have chosen us, and he concluded with: What are we, what are our lives? And praised him. Rav Huna, son of Rav Natan, said: And an individual says it after his prayer.
Rav said: The ne’ila prayer exempts the evening prayer. Rav to his reasoning as he said: It is an added prayer, and since he has prayed it he needs no further

And did Rav say this? Didn’t Rav say: halakha is in accordance with the statement of the one who says evening prayer is optional? He said this in accordance with the statement of the one who says is mandatory.

raises an objection evening Yom Kippur, one prays seven and confesses; morning prayer, seven and confesses; additional prayer, seven and confesses; during ne’ila one prays seven and confesses; the evening prayer, seven in abridged eighteen Rabbi Ḥanina ben Gamliel in the name of his ancestors: One prays full eighteen
